questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a test plan?
options: To execute test cases and record pass/fail results
To outline the testing strategy, scope, schedule, and responsibilities
To create traceability links between bugs and requirements
To define the hardware configuration for test environments
questions.rationale: A test plan is a blueprint for testing that outlines the strategy, scope, schedule, and role responsibilities. It is written by the test lead and used by test engineers to guide test case creation. It does not involve execution or traceability setup directly.

questions.question: Which task may be the responsibility of a test lead?
options: Executing test runs and recording results
Writing test plans and managing other roles' tasks
Importing test cases from vendor Excel files
questions.rationale: Test leads write test plans, configure Codebeamer artifacts, and manage other roles' tasks and responsibilities. Executing tests is the tester's responsibility, and writing test cases is typically the test engineer's role.

questions.question: What is the difference between parent-child relationships and reference relationships in Codebeamer?
options: Parent-child relationships are for traceability; reference relationships are for organization
Parent-child relationships organize items within a single tracker; reference relationships provide traceability across trackers
Parent-child relationships can only be used with requirement trackers
There is no difference — they are the same concept
questions.rationale: Parent-child relationships are strictly organizational tools within a single tracker and do not create traceability or trigger suspected badges. Reference relationships (upstream/downstream) provide traceability across different trackers and trigger suspected badges when upstream items change.

questions.question: Why are test case trackers configured with a Verifies field?
options: To assign test cases to specific testers for execution
To establish traceability between test cases and the requirements they validate
To organize test cases into parent-child hierarchies
To define the test environment configuration
questions.rationale: The Verifies field establishes a traceability link between a test case and the requirement it validates. This upstream/downstream relationship is what enables Codebeamer to track test coverage and flag suspected items when requirements change.
